prompt: Kuukikan (空気感) style hyper-realistic film photograph. California suburban home kitchen, early summer morning. A stainless-steel refrigerator door is half-open, revealing a perfectly organized interior: yogurt containers, fresh salmon fillet, green vegetables, fruits — everything neatly arranged on glass shelves. No people present. The kitchen is quiet and still. A large window on the right lets in soft, diffused morning light that spills across the white marble countertop and gently illuminates the refrigerator interior, creating a translucent, clean quality. Light gray shadows are open and luminous — never dark. Color grading: neutral, slightly desaturated film-like tones. Whites are clean but not blown. Natural food colors peek through the muted palette. Composition: 16:9 wide frame. The refrigerator occupies the left third. The remaining two-thirds are generous negative space — the sunlit window, white kitchen cabinets, and countertop providing openness and airiness. The subject (fridge) is not centered, creating asymmetry. On the glass refrigerator shelves, an extremely subtle prismatic spectral refraction (faint rainbow hues of pink and blue) appears on the edge — barely noticeable, a hidden accent of freshness. Camera: 35mm prime lens equivalent, f/4.0, ISO 200. Authentic analog film grain (fine, ISO 200 level). Soft old-lens peripheral bokeh and subtle vignette. The overall mood is serene completion — 'Everything is here, and no one had to do anything.' A still, hopeful, refreshing moment of daily life. Do not add dramatic backlighting, lens flares, or heavy contrast. Do not beautify or stylize. Keep the scene grounded and quietly luminous.
negative_prompt: dramatic lighting, lens flare, HDR, oversaturated colors, CGI rendering, plastic look, studio lighting, artificial glow, neon, high contrast, dark shadows, people, human figures, text overlays, watermarks, logos, AI smoothing, digital painting style, anime, cartoon, illustration
